# PUB003_DUPLICATE_TERMS

**Document ID:** CASE-PUB-003-DT

**Title:** Duplicate Terms

**Document Class:** Research Draft

**Status:** Draft

**Author:** SvitloSk Project — Architectural Investigation

---

# 1. Purpose

This document determines which terms are duplicated.

---

# 2. Duplicate Analysis

## 2.1 Publication vs Message vs Post

### Publication

**Definition:** "A public information message generated by the SvitloSk system." (GLOSSARY §3)

**Scope:** Channel-independent concept.

### Message

**Definition:** A Telegram-specific output.

**Scope:** Channel-specific.

### Post

**Definition:** A social media output (Telegram, Facebook).

**Scope:** Channel-specific.

### Relationship

| Term | Relationship |
|------|--------------|
| Publication → Message | Publication BECOMES a Message when delivered to Telegram |
| Publication → Post | Publication BECOMES a Post when delivered to Facebook |
| Message ≠ Post | Different channels, different terms |

### Verdict

**PARTIALLY DUPLICATE** — Publication is the abstract concept; Message/Post are channel-specific instantiations.

---

## 2.2 Publication vs Information Product

### Publication

**Definition:** "A public information message generated by the SvitloSk system." (GLOSSARY §3)

**Scope:** Single message.

### Information Product

**Definition:** A discrete unit of information produced by SvitloSk.

**Scope:** May be composite (e.g., Queue Graph).

### Relationship

| Term | Relationship |
|------|--------------|
| Information Product → Publication | Information Product may contain one or more Publications |
| Publication → Information Product | Publication is a type of Information Product |

### Verdict

**OVERLAPPING** — Information Product is broader; Publication is a specific type.

---

## 2.3 Edition vs Issue vs Release

### Edition

**Definition:** One daily instance of the Journal.

**Scope:** Used in Architect Intent.

### Issue

**Definition:** "An Issue opens when the first Publication for a calendar day is generated." (TELEGRAM_LIFECYCLE.md)

**Scope:** Telegram-specific lifecycle concept.

### Release

**Definition:** Used in CASE-PUB-001 as "composite entity."

**Scope:** Undefined in canonical documents.

### Relationship

| Term | Relationship |
|------|--------------|
| Edition ≈ Issue | Both refer to one daily instance |
| Release ≈ Edition | Both refer to one daily instance |
| Edition, Issue, Release | Potential synonyms |

### Verdict

**POTENTIALLY DUPLICATE** — Three terms for the same concept.

---

## 2.4 Journal vs Journal Edition

### Journal

**Definition:** "The SvitloSk Public Information Journal is the primary public communication channel." (CHARTER §10)

**Scope:** Ongoing service.

### Journal Edition

**Definition:** One daily instance of the Journal.

**Scope:** Daily container.

### Relationship

| Term | Relationship |
|------|--------------|
| Journal → Journal Edition | Journal is instantiated as Journal Editions |
| Journal Edition → Journal | Journal Edition is one instance of Journal |

### Verdict

**NOT DUPLICATE** — Different abstraction levels.

---

## 2.5 Graph vs Image vs PNG

### Graph

**Definition:** Visual representation of queue schedule.

**Scope:** Abstract concept.

### Image

**Definition:** Visual file (PNG, SVG).

**Scope:** File format.

### PNG

**Definition:** Portable Network Graphics format.

**Scope:** Specific file format.

### Relationship

| Term | Relationship |
|------|--------------|
| Graph → Image | Graph is rendered as an Image |
| Image → PNG | Image may be in PNG format |
| Graph → PNG | Graph may be saved as PNG |

### Verdict

**NOT DUPLICATE** — Different abstraction levels.

---

## 2.6 Notification vs Push Notification

### Notification

**Definition:** General concept of alerting residents.

**Scope:** General.

### Push Notification

**Definition:** "Push notifications belong ONLY to Queue Schedule." (Architect Intent)

**Scope:** Specific delivery mechanism.

### Relationship

| Term | Relationship |
|------|--------------|
| Notification → Push Notification | Push Notification is a type of Notification |
| Push Notification → Notification | Push Notification is a specific implementation |

### Verdict

**NOT DUPLICATE** — Different abstraction levels.

---

# 3. Duplicate Summary

| Term Set | Relationship | Verdict |
|----------|--------------|---------|
| Publication, Message, Post | Abstract → Channel-specific | PARTIALLY DUPLICATE |
| Publication, Information Product | Atomic → Composite | OVERLAPPING |
| Edition, Issue, Release | Same concept | POTENTIALLY DUPLICATE |
| Journal, Journal Edition | Service → Instance | NOT DUPLICATE |
| Graph, Image, PNG | Concept → Format → File | NOT DUPLICATE |
| Notification, Push Notification | General → Specific | NOT DUPLICATE |

---

# 4. Findings

## 4.1 Finding DT-001

**One term set is POTENTIALLY DUPLICATE.**

Edition, Issue, Release may refer to the same concept.

**Evidence:** Analysis of duplicate terms.

**Confidence:** HIGH.

## 4.2 Finding DT-002

**One term set is OVERLAPPING.**

Publication and Information Product overlap.

**Evidence:** Analysis of duplicate terms.

**Confidence:** HIGH.

## 4.3 Finding DT-003

**One term set is PARTIALLY DUPLICATE.**

Publication, Message, Post overlap at different abstraction levels.

**Evidence:** Analysis of duplicate terms.

**Confidence:** HIGH.

---

| Finding | Evidence Source |
|---------|-----------------|
| DT-001 | Analysis of duplicate terms |

---

**End of Duplicate Terms**
